description: Machine-readable metadata for AI agents working in the wo-library monorepo.

@wo-library/js.description: Pure TypeScript utilities with no browser or React dependencies.

array.description: Create numeric ranges; add-or-update items in arrays.

number.description: Number formatting using en-IN locale (Lakhs/Crores). formatNumber is the primary utility.
warnings: Always use en-IN locale — do NOT change to en-US.

objects.description: Push to array on object key (creating if absent); safely read deeply nested values.

colors.description: Convert between RGB and hex colors; generate random CSS gradients.

utils.description: Check for empty values (null/undefined/empty string/array); check if any items match a sub-array.

language.description: Conditionally pluralise a word based on count.

path.description: Normalise file paths to POSIX (forward-slash) format.

svg.description: Compute SVG arc path data from polar coordinates.

@wo-library/web.description: Browser and DOM utilities for networking, storage, script loading, and CSS.

fetch.description: Configurable HTTP client. Extend WoFetch and call getUrl/postUrl/patchUrl/putUrl/deleteUrl.

storage.description: Unified localStorage/sessionStorage/memory abstraction. Use anyStorageInstance for the singleton.

script-loading.description: Dynamically inject external scripts and stylesheets with CSP nonce support.

css.description: Read and set CSS custom properties on DOM elements.

svg.description: Serialise SVG nodes or markup strings to data URIs.

errors.description: Typed error hierarchy. WoResponseError carries data and HTTP status.

routing.description: Redirect to logout path with optional from/message query params.

payments.description: Open Razorpay checkout modal. Loads SDK dynamically.

@wo-library/react.description: React components (atomic design) and hooks. Minimal styling, composable with any design system.

atoms.description: Primitive building blocks. All accept className and standard HTML props.

items.Button: Interactive button. Props: variant, size, spacing, isBusy, isFullWidth, iconBefore, iconAfter, effects (ripple/cursor-tracking).
items.TextInput: Text input with label, icons, error state. Props: label, variant, size, hasError, isBusy, isLoading, iconBefore, iconAfter.
items.NumberInput: Number input using en-IN locale formatting. Extends FormattedInput.
items.Modal: Accessible dialog via @floating-ui/react. Props: isOpen, onClose, children.
items.Card: Polymorphic container. Props: as, variant, layout, floating, image, isBusy.
items.Image: Polymorphic image with aspect ratio. Props: as, aspectRatio, variant (basic/circular), isBusy, isLoading.
items.Text: Text block with line clamping. Props: inline, maxLines, minLines, isBusy, isLoading.
items.Title: Heading wrapper. Props: as, variant (tinyline/tinyline-left), align.
items.Container: Layout container. Props: spacing, width, height, align, variant.
items.Anchor: Polymorphic link. Props: as, variant, size, spacing, iconBefore, iconAfter, noVisited.
items.Tag: Inline badge/label. Props: iconBefore, iconAfter, isBusy, isLoading.
items.Spinner: Animated SVG spinner. Accepts span props.
items.Label: Form label element.
items.ObserveInView: Triggers callback when element enters viewport (IntersectionObserver).
items.LoaderWrapper: Shows a spinner while isBusy is true.

items.CardLink: Card styled as a clickable link.
items.Popover: Floating popover with trigger and content.

hooks.useMethods: Type-safe useReducer with named methods. Returns [state, { method1, method2 }].
hooks.useCalendly: Loads Calendly SDK and returns a function to open the popup widget.
hooks.useScrollDirection: Returns { direction: 'up' | 'down' | '', x, y } updated on scroll.
hooks.useStateWithProp: Like useState but auto-syncs when the prop changes.
hooks.usePropRef: Merges forwardedRef(s) into a single innerRef + setInnerRef callback.
hooks.useMeasureInput: Returns [labelRef, extraStyles] for multi-line label height compensation.

ThemeContext.description: Manages active theme name and class name. Use ThemeProvider at the app root.

LayoutContext.description: Tracks layout dimensions, refs for topNav/sideNav, and breakpoints.

contexts.createAndUseContext: Utility to create split state+dispatch context pairs.

@wo-library/ui.description: CSS styles, design-system themes, and SVG path data.

contents.base.css: CSS reset and base design tokens.

patterns.namedExportsOnly: All public APIs use named exports. Never add default exports to public APIs.
patterns.typescriptStrict: TypeScript strict mode is enabled. Use 'unknown' instead of 'any'.
patterns.indiaLocale: Number formatting uses en-IN (Indian numbering system: Lakhs/Crores). Never change to en-US.
patterns.cssModules: All component styles use *.module.css. No inline styles.
patterns.polymorphicAs: Many components accept an 'as' prop to change the rendered HTML element.

busyVsLoading.isBusy: Background operation in progress — content visible, spinner overlaid.
busyVsLoading.isLoading: Content not yet available — replaced with skeleton loader.

patterns.splitContext: State and dispatch contexts are always split into two providers to prevent unnecessary re-renders.
patterns.useMethods: Prefer useMethods over useReducer for readable named-action state management.

testing.imports: Import from '@jest/globals', not the jest global
testing.location: Test files go in __tests__/ subdirectories alongside source

buildSystem.typescript: Compiled via Babel (not tsc direct) for builds

documentation.primaryHub: Storybook (packages/storybook). All docs live in one place.
documentation.componentDocs: Storybook autodocs (autodocs: true) automatically extracts JSDoc from TypeScript component props via react-docgen-typescript.

apiDocs.description: Generates markdown API reference for all packages and outputs it as .mdx files into packages/storybook/src/docs/. These are picked up by Storybook's stories glob and appear as API reference pages alongside component stories.

documentation.ciWorkflowDescription: On push to main: build packages → pnpm docs (TypeDoc) → build-storybook → deploy to GitHub Pages.

storybookHierarchy.Atoms/*: Individual UI primitives (Button, TextInput, Modal, etc.)
storybookHierarchy.API/*: TypeDoc-generated API reference for utilities, hooks, and types
